text: A general rule for organisations ending in 'Versicherung' is missing entirely. Add a rule: one or more capitalised words followed by 'Versicherung', similar to the Finanzamt/FA pattern.

The rule misses hyphenated location names like 'Salzburg-Stadt' and 'Niederösterreich Mitte'. The location part of the pattern must allow hyphens within words, e.g. each word should optionally be followed by (-Word). Currently the rule also produces truncated false positives like 'FA Salzburg' instead of 'FA Salzburg-Stadt' because it stops at the hyphen.

Three gaps causing misses: 1) All-caps abbreviation prefixes like 'KQPC' are not matched, allow [A-Z]{2,6} as a valid name start. 2) Names containing '&' like 'Klusner&Päffgen Bildung GMBH' are not matched, allow & as a connector between name parts. 3) CamelCase compound words like 'UnterRecycling' are not matched because the pattern requires lowercase after the first capital.

Same hyphen problem as FA rule. Names like 'Talkel-Versand AG', 'Hebebrand Recycling AG', 'Logseemon-Metall AG' are all missed because the pattern stops at hyphens. This also causes truncated FPs like 'Versand AG' or 'Metall AG'. Allow hyphens within the name component.
